mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which weakens corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal a tightening monetary environment that raises equity discount rates and reduces valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS exceed thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 60-day moving average signals weakening supply-side activity, which reduces corporate profits and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO falls below 105.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) in the prior quarter indicates robust economic demand, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of quarters with GDP growth above 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,000 units signals weakening demand for construction, which reduces economic activity and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST falls below 1000.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and declines in unemployment (UNRATE) signal a strong labor market, which boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 140k and UNRATE falls below 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 0.1% during a high-inflation environment (CPIAUCSL > 235) signals aggressive monetary tightening, which raises borrowing costs and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.1% and CPIAUCSL is above 235.
